_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
alos: 2 days (Once diagnosis is established the case can be booked in the relevant package, further stay/admission should be decided based on the level of complications of the disease)
clinical_indications: Rapid fatigue of muscles distinguishing myasthenia from other neuromuscular disorders, with progressive worsening over the day or with repetitive activity
Involvement of respiration and swallowing musculature

preauth.label: Clinical notes showing vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment and advice for admission

Evidence of decremental response on repetitive nerve conduction studies (EMG/ENMG) / Prostigmine/Tensilon test – documentation of transient improvement in condition / Ach receptor antibody testing – positive

claim.label: Detailed ICPs with detailed line of treatment
Progress of the patient in terms of clinical condition; decreases fatigue/vision improvement
Detailed Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge

ppd_preauth.q: Is there a h/o rapid fatigue of muscles ie repeated activities increases the weakness/ evening ptosis/vision problems in the evening?

Is there evidence of decremental response on repetitive nerve conduction studies (EMG/ENMG) / documentation of transient improvement with Prostigmine/Tensilon test / positive Ach receptor antibody testing?

cpd_claim.q: Is progress documented in terms of decreased fatigue/vision improvement?

common_queries: History of rapid muscle fatigue (repeated activities increasing weakness/evening ptosis/vision problems in the evening) not documented.
Clinical notes, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment, or advice for admission are missing or incomplete.

Nerve conduction studies (EMG/ENMG), Prostigmine/Tensilon test, or Ach receptor antibody testing results are not provided, or do not show decremental response, transient improvement, or positive antibody test.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with treatment details are missing or incomplete.
Documentation of patient progress (decreases fatigue/vision improvement) is absent or insufficient.
Detailed discharge summary or follow-up advice is missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Myasthenia gravis (MG) is an autoimmune disease in which antibodies are directed at the postsynaptic membrane of the neuromuscular junction, leading to varying degrees of muscle weakness and fatigability.
Where MG presents before 19 years of age, it is termed juvenile myasthenia gravis (JMG).
JMG mostly presents in late infancy and childhood. Commonly affected muscles are eyes, face and swallowing.
Proceed with JMG only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation.
JMG is primarily a clinical diagnosis with classical patterns of fluctuating weakness and fatigability.
Detection of antibodies to the acetylcholine receptor (AChR) supports the diagnosis of JMG.
